Nigeria's private investment infrastructure

Where serious capital meets verified business growth.

CoFund is a curated deal-flow platform connecting verified investors with credible, growth-ready businesses. Every opportunity is due-diligence cleared. Every investment is structured, tracked, and protected.

How CoFund works

Private investment infrastructure. Built for serious deal-making.

01
Verify your identity
Register and complete KYC (BVN/NIN). Only verified investors access deal flow. This protects every stakeholder on the platform.
02
Access curated deal flow
Browse only due-diligence-cleared opportunities — SMEs, real estate, agro-processing, and asset-backed deals. No speculation. No unverified listings.
03
Invest in structured deals
Capital enters milestone-based escrow. Funds are deployed in controlled tranches — not handed over in one lump sum. Accountability built in from day one.
04
Track, earn, and grow
Monitor milestones and payout progress in real time. Returns are tied to verified business revenue — not promises. No revenue, no payout.

Live opportunities

Verified deals, open now

Every listing has cleared CoFund due diligence. Background check report and full financials available before you commit a naira.

Verification system

Background checks protect your investment

Every business goes through our verification partner. See exactly what has been checked before investing. If a project is unverified, you can request a check yourself.

FULL
Fully Verified
CAC registration, director identity (BVN/NIN), business address, 2-year financial records, and full AML screening — all independently verified by CoFund Verify. Highest confidence for investors.
BASIC
Basic Verified
CAC registration and director identity confirmed. Basic AML screening completed. Core checks passed — financial records and address pending deeper review. Suitable for initial investment consideration.
PREM
Premium Verified
Everything in Fully Verified plus physical site inspection, third-party financial audit, and CEO background check. The highest standard available on CoFund. Full report accessible to Pro and Elite plan subscribers.

Founder Accountability

Founders invest in their own projects

Every project on CoFund requires the founder to invest their own capital. Aligned interests. Real commitment. Higher investor confidence.

SME Projects
5–15% personal investment
Founder must contribute 5–15% of funding target from personal capital. Displayed on project page. Boosts trust score.
Real Estate
10–25% personal investment
Higher contribution required — property development demands maximum founder alignment. Verified before listing.
Asset-Backed
10–20% personal investment
Collateral-backed projects still require founder skin-in-the-game. Asset + contribution = maximum security.
Startups
Flexible — strong commitment required
Startups may have lower capital floors but must demonstrate strong commitment through sweat equity or milestone pledges.
Platform Rule: Founders are expected to invest in their own projects. Higher personal contribution boosts their Trust Score and increases investor confidence. Contribution amount is mandatory and displayed publicly on every project page.

Join CoFund

Ready to deploy capital — or raise it?

Together, We Grow

Curated deal flow

Verified investment opportunities

Every listing has passed CoFund due diligence. Click View Details to read the full verification report, financial records, and structured return model before committing.

CoFund ROI Rule — No returns before revenue
All projects on CoFund pay returns only when the business earns real revenue. Zero returns during construction, pre-launch, or inactive phases. This is how CoFund protects investors from phantom promises.

CoFund Pro

Invest smarter.
See what others can't.

Pro gives you full due diligence reports, deal analytics, early access to new listings, direct founder messaging, and live milestone tracking — everything a serious private investor needs to make confident, structured decisions.

What you unlock

9 features built for serious investors

Free users see the surface. Pro users see everything underneath.

Advanced Due Diligence

Full verification reports: financial summaries, risk ratings, founder background checks, site inspection notes — the complete picture before you invest.

Free users see:
Basic project info only
Pro users see:
Full financial report + risk score
Smart Investment Insights

AI-powered projections: estimated payout timelines, risk vs return analysis, best/worst case scenarios. "Est. time to 1.5× return: 12–16 months"

Example insight:
At ₦22M/month revenue, you reach 1.42× in ~14 months. Low probability of delay based on milestone track record.
48h Early Access

Pro users see new projects 48 hours before public listing. The best deals fill fast — be first in line before the general investor pool opens.

Why it matters:
Agri-Export's last round was 64% funded within 72 hours of public listing. Early access = best allocation.
Direct Founder Access

Direct messaging to project founders is a Pro-only feature. Free plan investors receive monthly written reports from businesses. Pro users get full two-way messaging — ask questions, request updates, and communicate directly with the team running your investment.

Private messaging to any project founder
Founder response time displayed on their profile
Live Project Tracking

Visual milestone tracker shows exactly where your investment is right now — construction phase, revenue active, delays flagged. No more wondering "when does ROI start?"

Phase 1 complete — funding closed
Phase 2 active — operations underway
Phase 3 locked — awaiting revenue trigger
Secondary Market Access

Buy stakes from exiting investors or list your own. Pro users get 2-day processing (vs 5 days free). Elite users get same-day transfers. The only way to exit before maturity.

Free plan transfer speed5 business days
Pro plan2 business days
Elite planSame day
Priority Investor Status

Pro investors get slight priority in oversubscribed rounds. When demand exceeds supply, Pro allocations are confirmed first. Better access to the most competitive deals.

Priority queue in oversubscribed projects
Better access to limited-slot rounds
Trust Score Breakdown

See the full trust score breakdown for any project or investor — repayment history, revenue performance rating, milestone delivery score, risk profile. Know exactly what you're investing in.

Project trust score details
Revenue performance rating
Founder credibility score
Verification Credits

Verification reports are credit-gated. Free users pay ₦5,000/report. Pro plans include 2 credits/month. Elite plans include unlimited credits. Credits do not roll over.

Free plan₦5,000 per check
Pro plan2 free/month
Elite planUnlimited

CoFund Verify

3 levels of business verification

Every business on CoFund goes through our verification process. The level determines what investors can see.

Basic Verified
CAC registration + director identity confirmed. Basic AML screening done. Visible to all users.
Included: CAC · Director identity
Fully Verified
Full CAC, address, BVN, 2-year financials, AML, fraud screen, litigation check. Full report visible to Pro users.
Includes: All basic + financial records + address + full AML
Premium Verified
Everything in Fully Verified plus physical site inspection, 3rd-party audit, CEO background check. Full report to Elite users.
Includes: All + site inspection + 3rd-party audit
Verification fee: ₦50,000 · Fully credited toward platform success fee when funded

Returns Overview

All CoFund investments use the Capped Revenue Share model. Returns are tied to real business cash flow.

How returns work

You earn a percentage of the business's actual revenue until your total payout reaches the agreed cap multiple (e.g. 1.42×). No revenue from the business = no payout to you.

Return phases
Pre-revenue — ₦0 to investors. Business is operational but hasn't hit income yet.
Revenue active — Revenue share distributed quarterly from verified receipts.
Cap reached — Total payouts hit the agreed multiple. Distributions stop.

Secondary Marketplace

To exit an investment before maturity, transfer your stake to another verified CoFund investor.

ℹ️ No early withdrawal. The Secondary Market is the only way to exit before maturity. This protects businesses and fellow investors. List your stake here and another investor buys it from you.

How it works

1️⃣List your stake

Set a price for your investment position. Other investors can see it and express interest.

2️⃣CoFund facilitates the transfer

Once a buyer is matched and payment confirmed, your stake is transferred. Processing: Free (5d), Pro (2d), Elite (same day).

Legal

Terms & Conditions

Last updated: April 10, 2026 · CoFund Ltd., Lagos, Nigeria · Registered with the Corporate Affairs Commission

1. Platform overview

CoFund is a digital investment and crowdfunding marketplace connecting verified investors, business owners, and strategic partners. CoFund operates as a marketplace and intermediary — it does not guarantee investment returns. By using CoFund you agree to these Terms in full. CoFund operates under applicable Nigerian SEC crowdfunding regulations and AML/CFT compliance frameworks.

2. Eligibility and accounts

You must be at least 18 years old and a verified Nigerian resident or citizen with a valid BVN and NIN to invest on CoFund. All users must complete KYC verification before any financial transaction. CoFund reserves the right to refuse, suspend, or terminate any account that violates these Terms, poses a fraud risk, or fails repeated KYC checks.

3. Investment types and tiers

Standard investments (Fixed Return, Revenue Share): available to all verified investors at the project's stated minimum investment amount.

Capped Revenue Share ( Elite): premium investment positions with higher ROI bonuses, direct business access, and priority payouts. Revenue share rates are agreed per project — he project's total funding target, scaled by project category:
• SME: Bronze from ~10% of target · Elite based on actual cash flow
• Real Estate: Bronze from ~10% of target · Elite based on actual cash flow
• Startup: Bronze from ~10% of target · Elite based on actual cash flow
• Asset-backed: Bronze from ~10% of target · Elite based on actual cash flow

Capped Revenue Share investments are commercial arrangements governed by a signed investment agreement. They do not confer registered shareholding, directorship, or ownership rights unless separately contracted in writing.

4. Background checks and verification

Businesses may carry one of four verification statuses: Fully verified or Not verified. Verification status is displayed clearly on every project listing and detail page.

Investors may request a background check on any project through CoFund Verify, our licensed verification partner. Cost: ₦5,000 per check (Free plan); 2 free per month (Pro); unlimited (Elite). Background check reports are displayed in the project's Documents section. CoFund does not guarantee the completeness of third-party background check results. Background check approval is not a guarantee of investment safety.

5. Subscribe feature

Users may "Subscribe" to any project to receive updates in their Community inbox without making a financial commitment. Subscribing is free and does not constitute an investment or reservation. Users can unsubscribe at any time.

6. Escrow and milestone releases

All investor funds are held by CoFund's licensed escrow partner and are released to businesses only after admin-verified milestones. The default release schedule is 30% / 40% / 30% corresponding to project phases. If a funding target is not met within the campaign window, 100% of invested funds are automatically refunded within 5 business days. No platform fees are charged on unfunded projects.

7. Secondary Market — the only exit route

CoFund does not provide early withdrawal of invested funds. The Secondary Marketplace is the only mechanism for investors to exit a position before maturity. An investor may list their stake for sale on the Secondary Marketplace; once a verified buyer is matched and payment confirmed, the stake is transferred. Transfer processing times: Free plan — 5 business days; Pro plan — 2 business days; Elite plan — same business day. Revenue share model positions listed on the Secondary Market transfer the associated tier benefits to the buyer, subject to CoFund approval. Repeatedly listing positions may affect your Trust Score.

8. Platform fees

CoFund charges a 2% success fee on fully funded rounds (paid by the business upon target achievement). No fees are charged on investments held to maturity or on unfunded projects. CoFund Pro subscription fees (₦4,999/month or ₦49,999/year) and CoFund Elite fees (₦15,000/month) are non-refundable after the 7-day trial period. Secondary Marketplace transfer fee: 1% of transfer value, charged to the seller.

9. Premium plans and badges

CoFund Pro and CoFund Elite are subscription tiers that unlock additional platform features including early access, direct messaging, free background checks, and higher revenue share models. Badges (Pro Investor, Elite Plan, Bronze, Silver, Gold, Top Performer) are awarded based on verified platform activity and plan status. Badges are non-transferable, non-sellable, and may be revoked if qualifying conditions are no longer met. Misrepresenting badge status to other users or businesses is a violation of these Terms.

10. Trust Score

Trust Scores are dynamic and reflect verified platform data. Fraud, failed KYC, project defaults, or repeated low-quality behaviour will reduce scores. Scores below 60 result in project suspension. A score of 0 triggers mandatory account review and possible ban. CoFund reserves the right to adjust the Trust Score algorithm at any time with 30 days' notice.

11. Investment risk disclosure

All investments carry risk. Returns are not guaranteed. Past performance of listed projects is not indicative of future results. CoFund does not provide financial advice. You should seek independent professional advice before making any investment. Only invest funds you can afford to lose. Background check approval is not a guarantee of investment safety.

12. Community and messaging

The CoFund Community hub is a moderated space. Users must not share fraudulent, offensive, or misleading content. Direct business messaging is restricted to Pro and Elite plan members. CoFund reserves the right to remove messages, ban users, or report content to relevant authorities. Businesses may not solicit off-platform investments through the messaging system.

13. Governing law and disputes

These Terms are governed by Nigerian law. All disputes are subject to Nigerian jurisdiction. CoFund acts as a marketplace and is not a party to investment agreements between investors and businesses. Fraud concerns should be reported immediately to [email protected]. CoFund will cooperate fully with EFCC, NFIU, and SEC investigations.

Legal

Privacy Policy

Last updated: April 10, 2026 · CoFund Ltd., Lagos, Nigeria

1. Data we collect

  • Account data: name, email, phone number, password (hashed)
  • Identity data: BVN, NIN, date of birth, state of residence
  • KYC documents: government ID images, selfie (processed by KYC partner only)
  • Financial data: bank account details, investment records, transaction history
  • Platform data: login times, pages visited, subscription status, Trust Score activity
  • Background check requests and results (handled by CoFund Verify)

2. How we use your data

  • Identity verification and KYC compliance
  • Processing investments, escrow, and returns
  • Fraud prevention and AML/CFT compliance
  • Trust Score calculation and badge assignment
  • Platform communications (notifications, Community messages, updates)
  • Aggregate analytics to improve CoFund (anonymised)

We do not sell, rent, or share your personal data with third parties for marketing purposes.

3. KYC partner

Identity verification is handled exclusively by our licensed KYC partner (Smile Identity / Youverify). Raw identity documents are NOT stored by CoFund — only the verification result and compliance flags. All data is encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3).

4. Background check data

Background check requests are forwarded to CoFund Verify. Reports are stored securely and displayed only to the requesting investor and CoFund admin. We do not share check results with third parties outside compliance obligations. Results are retained for up to 7 years per AML/CFT regulations.

5. Community and messaging

When you subscribe to a project, the business owner sees your aggregate subscriber count only — not your name or investment amount unless you share it in group chat. Direct messages (Pro/Elite) are visible to the business owner. CoFund moderators may review flagged content.

6. Cookies and tracking

CoFund uses essential cookies for authentication and session management. We use analytics cookies (anonymised) to improve the platform. You may disable non-essential cookies in your browser settings. We do not use advertising cookies or third-party tracking pixels.

7. Data retention

  • Account data: retained while account is active, deleted within 30 days of account deletion request
  • KYC and transaction records: retained for 7 years per Nigerian AML/CFT regulations (cannot be deleted during this period)
  • Communication data: retained for 3 years
  • Anonymised analytics: retained indefinitely

8. Your rights

  • Access: Request a copy of your personal data
  • Correction: Request correction of inaccurate data
  • Export: Request your data in a portable format
  • Deletion: Request deletion (subject to regulatory retention requirements)
  • Objection: Object to processing for analytics purposes

Contact: [email protected] · Response within 14 business days.

9. Security

CoFund uses AES-256 encryption at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit, multi-factor authentication for admin access, and regular penetration testing by third-party security firms. In the event of a data breach affecting your personal data, we will notify you within 72 hours of becoming aware of the breach, as required by applicable law.

10. Contact us

Data Protection Officer: [email protected]
Privacy enquiries: [email protected]

Investor Education

Learn before you invest

Understanding how CoFund works protects your money. Read these key concepts before investing a single naira.

Revenue Share Model
You invest → business earns real revenue → a % of that revenue is distributed to you → once you reach the cap (e.g. 1.4×), distributions stop. No guarantees, no phantom returns.
Escrow & Milestone Release
Your funds are held in escrow — not released to the business in one lump sum. Capital is released in phases only when verified milestones are met. Fraud protection built-in.
Secondary Market Exit
There is no early withdrawal. If you need to exit before maturity, list your stake on the Secondary Market. Another investor buys it from you. Your original investment is not refunded by CoFund.
Verification Levels
Not all projects are equal. Fully Verified means CAC, BVN, address, financials, and AML checks passed. Always check a project's verification level and trust score before investing.
Risk Labels
Every project carries a risk label (Low / Medium / High). Startups are higher risk. Asset-backed projects are lower risk. Diversify your portfolio across risk levels and sectors.
Founder Skin-in-the-Game
Founders must invest their own money in their projects. This reduces fraud risk and shows real commitment. Always check the founder contribution % on the project page — higher is better.

Support

Help Centre & FAQ

Find answers about how CoFund works, investments, security, and your account.

What is the Minimum Performance Trigger?▾
Some projects have a minimum performance trigger — a revenue threshold the business must hit before investor payouts begin. For example, a restaurant must reach ₦2M/month revenue before distributions start. This prevents micro-payments and ensures every payout is meaningful. The trigger is shown on each project's return model section.
What happens if a business delays too long before generating revenue?▾
CoFund's Time + Performance Hybrid clause protects investors. If a business significantly delays hitting its revenue milestone, the clause activates — triggering a renegotiation, partial return of funds, or admin mediation. Penalty clauses apply to the business, not to investors. All milestone deadlines are tracked live on your portfolio dashboard.
How does the business verification fee credit work?▾
Businesses pay a ₦50,000 verification fee to get their CoFund Verify badge. This fee is not refunded — but it is fully credited toward CoFund's platform success fee when the project gets funded. Example: if a business raises ₦10M, CoFund charges a 5% success fee = ₦500,000. The ₦50,000 verification fee is deducted from this, so CoFund collects ₦450,000 net. The business effectively pays ₦0 extra for verification if they raise successfully.
What is CoFund and how does it work?▾
CoFund is a Nigerian investment marketplace connecting verified investors with businesses seeking growth capital. You browse verified projects, choose a Capped Revenue Share model, invest any amount above the project minimum, and your funds are held in milestone-based escrow. Returns only begin once the business earns real revenue — no phantom guarantees.
What is the minimum amount I can invest?▾
Each project sets its own minimum. Standard investments start from as low as ₦50,000 (e.g. FreshBake Industrial). Revenue share model investments start at 10% of the project's funding target — for a ₦20M SME, that's ₦2M for Revenue share. Always check the project detail page for exact minimums.
What happens if a project doesn't reach its funding target?▾
If a project fails to reach its target within the campaign window, 100% of your invested capital is automatically returned to your CoFund wallet within 5 business days. No fees are charged. This is enforced at the escrow level — CoFund cannot access unfunded capital.
How do I exit an investment before maturity?▾
The Secondary Marketplace is the only way to exit before maturity. There is no direct early withdrawal — this protects other investors and the business. You list your stake at your chosen price, a matched buyer purchases it, and CoFund facilitates the transfer. Processing time depends on your plan: Free (5 days), Pro (2 days), Elite (same day).
What are the different return types on CoFund?▾
1. Fixed Return — You earn a guaranteed percentage per year (e.g. 19% p.a.), paid quarterly or monthly regardless of business performance. The rate is agreed upfront and locked in. Lowest risk.

2. Revenue Share — You earn a percentage of the business's actual revenue each quarter (e.g. 8% of quarterly revenue). Returns vary with business performance. Higher potential upside but not guaranteed.

Use the live ROI calculator in the invest modal to see projected returns for each type before committing.
How is the ROI calculator projection worked out?▾
The calculator uses: Return = Amount × (Rate / 100) × (Duration in months / 12). For example: ₦500,000 at 19% p.a. for 12 months = ₦95,000 total return. Quarterly payout = ₦23,750. Revenue share models add their bonus rate on top. Revenue Share estimates are based on the projected rate — actual payouts depend on real revenue.
When and how do I receive my returns?▾
Returns are paid to your CoFund wallet after each admin-verified milestone. Revenue share projects pay quarterly or semi-annually (stated on the project page). Once in your wallet, you can withdraw to your verified bank account (1–3 business days) or reinvest into new projects.
Are returns guaranteed?▾
Revenue share rates are contractually agreed and backed by the escrow structure. However, no investment is entirely risk-free. If a business defaults, CoFund's protection fund covers up to 20% of principal. Revenue share model investors have priority recovery. Revenue share returns depend on actual revenue and are not guaranteed. Always review the business's background check, Trust Score, and documents before investing.
How does the escrow system protect me?▾
All investor funds are held by a licensed escrow partner — not by CoFund and not by the business. Funds are released in three phases (30% / 40% / 30%) only after CoFund admin verifies each milestone. If a project fails at any stage, remaining escrow is returned to investors. You can track every escrow release in real time under My Portfolio → Escrow Tracker.
What does a background check actually verify?▾
CoFund Verify (our licensed partner) checks: CAC registration, director identity via BVN/NIN, physical business address, 2-year audited financial records, AML/fraud screening, and litigation check. A Fully Verified badge means all checks passed. Not Verified means no check has been done — you can request one yourself for ₦5,000 (Free plan) or free with Pro/Elite plans.
How does KYC verification work?▾
KYC is required before investing. You submit your BVN, NIN, date of birth, state of residence, government ID (passport, NIN card, driver's licence, or voter's card), and a selfie holding your ID. Our partner (Smile Identity / Youverify) processes this within 24 hours. On approval you receive the Verified badge and a Trust Score of 50/100. CoFund stores only the verification result — not your raw documents.
What is the Trust Score?▾
Your Trust Score (0–100) reflects your identity verification, investment history, repayment behaviour, and platform engagement. Starting score after KYC is 50. It grows as you make investments, complete terms, leave reviews, and use the platform actively. Fraud, failed KYC attempts, or defaults reduce it. A score of 70+ unlocks the ⭐ Trusted Investor badge. View your full breakdown under My Portfolio → Trust Score.
Are CoFund investments the same as owning equity?▾
No. CoFund investments are revenue-share arrangements governed by a signed investment agreement. They do not confer registered shareholding, board voting rights, or ownership of the business. Any equity arrangement would be separately contracted outside CoFund's standard structure.

Still have questions?

Our support team typically responds within 2 business hours during Lagos business hours (8AM–6PM WAT).
